def: adj. Flowing, gliding along; brave, bold; public, notorious.
s.f. Pain, suffering, affliction, distress; resistance, opposition, obstruction, hindrance.
s. m. 1. A fowler. 2. The name of a tribe of Hindūs.
s.m. Hunter; fowler; armed retainer (especially one armed with a bow and arrows); gamekeeper ( = bahaliyā); name of a tribe of Rājpūts ( = baheriyā).
n. m. A fowler ( chiṛī-mār ); hunter.
s.m. = baheṛā, q.v.; — adj. = bahrā, q.v.
more: n. f. H. bhiṛnā to join. See ارَي A crowd; throng; multitude; swarm; crush. 1. Bhīṛ, amboh, aur bhaṛakke haiṅ. Dhūm dhauṅsoṅ kī, aur dhaṛakke haiṅ . Nazīr. 2. Chitr koṭ ke ghāṭ pe bhaī santan kī bhīṛ, Tulsī Dās chandan ghiseṅ, tilak det Raghbīr . Dohā. 3. Jāfar, ba-kūë-i-yār agar bhīṛ bhāṛ hai, Tū bhī ghusaṛ-pusaṛ-ke ghuseṛ apnī āp ko! Jāfar Zatallī. No matter if your mistress' lane be choked up with a throng, Do you too elbow, press, and shove, and force your way along! bhīṛ bhāṛ, bhīṛ bhaṛakkā , n. A crowd (بهيرَ). bhīṛ paṛnā , v. n. To be entangled in difficulties, troubles, or misfortunes. Bābū, durbal kāhe mātā toharā sarīr, Kaun paṛal tohrā par bhīṛ? Mag. Song. Sarvan. bhīṛ karnā, yā lagānā , v. a. To crowd; swarm.
adj. Narrow; close; tight; close-packed. bhīṛā , n. m. One who has contracted eyebrows.

def: s.m. Belleric myrobalan, Terminalia belerica.
n. m. The beleric myrobalan.
s. m. Beleric myrobalan. (Terminalia belerica).
s.m. Name of a clan of Rājpūts in Jaunpur and Chunar (cf. baheliyā).
s. m. A vagabond, vagrant, wanderer, rambler.
s.m. Wanderer, vagrant, vagabond.
s.m. A great disputer or arguer; controversialist.
more: adj. superl. (from بحث) A great disputer, controvertist, arguer.

def: s.f. Book of daily accounts, inerchant's or banker's book, ledger, register, diary, journal; book (not stitched at the sides but at the ends)
subs: bahī-khasrā (S. vr̤iddhi-kshata), s.f. Account books; ledger; set of books kept by merchants or bankers
v.t. To enter in the ledger, place to account, bring on the books, to debit or credit
wety: Borrowed from Persian بهی.

def: adj. = bāhya or vāhya, q.v. adj. & s.m. Outer, external, outward; relating to abroad, foreign, strange; — outsider, stranger, foreigner; one of a low caste.
adj. f. Barren. (Generally applied to cattle).
adj. Barren (generally applied to cattle).
s.f. Sister; female cousin; an affectionate term of address.
more: adj. S. भिद् to break. (Arith.) A fraction. bhinn bhinn , adv. Separately; severally; individually.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀪𑀇𑀡𑀺 (bhaïṇi), from Sanskrit भगिनी (bhaginī). Cognate with Punjabi بَھیْݨ (bhaiṇ).

def: s.f. Sister, &c. ( = bahin: com. to women; — also written baihnā; bhainā, bhenā).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀳𑀤𑀺 (vahadi), from Sanskrit वहति (váhati).

def: s.f. = vāhinī, q.v. s.f. An army, body of forces; a division of an army (consisting of 81 elephants, 81 cars, 243 horse, 405 foot); a battalion; a cohort; — a river
subs: s.m. Lord or leader of an army, a general, a commanding officer; — ʻlord of rivers,' an epithet of the ocean.

def: adj. Surpassing, excelling, eminent; conspicuous, manifest, open, public.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀩𑀸𑀳𑀺𑀭 (bāhira), from Sanskrit बहिर् (bahir), a compounding form of बहिस् (bahis).

def: s.f. = bahīr, q.v. & cf. bhīṛ. intj. Out! away!
subs: s.m. Outside and inside; ingress and egress; going out, visiting; — adv. In and out; within and without
v.n. To go out; to go away or on a journey
adv. Left out, excluded, excepted, exclusive of (see next)
v.t. To leave out, omit, exclude; to put out, turn out, eject, expel; to put away (a wife)
s.f. lit. 'Odour of the country,' rusticity, clownishness, boorishness
s.f. A woman who goes out into the streets and markets usually without a veil (in contradistinction to parde-wālī)
v.t. To take away; to take abroad, to export
(-se), To come out (of); to come outside; to go about in public; to be out or abroad; to withdraw (from)

(-se), To be outside (of), to be excluded (from); to be beyond the effect or influence (of), to disregard (e.g. wǒh to mere kahne-se bāhar hai); to dissent (from), disapprove (of), not to agree to (e.g. ham kisī bāt-se bāhar nahīṅ, 'I agree to everything').

def: n. f. H. bhīṛ crowd. 1. Camp followers; the baggage, &c. of an army. 2. Lines near a camp where the wives and families of the soldiers live. bahīṛ bungā, bhīṛ bhungā , n. f. P. buṅgāh a tent. The rabble of camp followers; the baggage or "impedimenta" of an army on the march.
more: s. f. 1. The baggage, &c. of an army: see بـﮩ‍يڙ bahīṛ. 2. See بهيڙ bhīṛ.

def: s. f. Baggage, &c. in an army.
The baggage of an army.
intj. Out! away!
more: adv. Without, out, outside, abroad, away; adj. Foreign.
part. act. (from بهر) Excelling, eminent; conspicuous, manifest.
subs: s.m. Outside and inside; ingress and egress; going out, visiting; — adv. In and out; within and without
v.n. To go out; to go away or on a journey
adv. Left out, excluded, excepted, exclusive of (see next)
v.t. To leave out, omit, exclude; to put out, turn out, eject, expel; to put away (a wife)
s.f. lit. 'Odour of the country,' rusticity, clownishness, boorishness
s.f. A woman who goes out into the streets and markets usually without a veil (in contradistinction to parde-wālī)
v.t. To take away; to take abroad, to export
(-se), To come out (of); to come outside; to go about in public; to be out or abroad; to withdraw (from)

(-se), To be outside (of), to be excluded (from); to be beyond the effect or influence (of), to disregard (e.g. wǒh to mere kahne-se bāhar hai); to dissent (from), disapprove (of), not to agree to (e.g. ham kisī bāt-se bāhar nahīṅ, 'I agree to everything').
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀩𑀸𑀳𑀺𑀭 (bāhira), from Sanskrit बहिर् (bahir), a compounding form of बहिस् (bahis).

ety: changeable to बहिस् bahis and बहिः bahih̥
def: adj. Out, without, outside, outer, ex ternal, exterior; foreign
subs: v.n. To come out, to issue forth (com. bāhar ānā)
adj. & s.m. Impious; impious person; neglect or violation of any moral or religious duty.

def: s.f. Baggage, &c. of an army; camp followers; lines near a camp where the wives and families of the soldiers live
subs: bahīr-o-buna (vulg. bhīṛ-bhungā), s.f. Baggage or impedimenta of an army on the march; rabble of camp-followers.

def: s.m. The off-side, the left side (in fencing, sword-exercise, &c.).
s.m. The off-side, the left side (in fencing, sword-exercise, &c.).
more: s. m. The left side (in sword exercise, &c.).

def: adj. Deaf; hard of hearing; heedless, inattentive.
wety: From Sanskrit बधिर (badhira).

def: v. n. To issue, come out.
more: (trans. of بـﮩرنا) v. a. To bring back, to cause to return.

def: v.t. = bahlānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be pleased, to amuse, divert, recreate, entertain, cheer, enliven (syn. bahirānā).
more: v. a. 1. To bring together; to cause to touch or be joined. 2. To bring in hostile contact; to set by the ears; foment a quarrel; cause a fight. Do ko bhiṛā dīyā, āp alag ho gaë . 3. To give a bribe. 4. To stake. bhiṛā-huā ; A. muttasil , adj. 1. Joined; touching; adjacent; at loggerheads. 2. Closed; shut; not open (a door). 3. Narrow; close; contracted; cramped.

def: s. m. The neglect or violation of any moral or religious duty; impious.
adv. With, or in, the computation; with a proper account (opp. to ʻalaʼl-ḥisāb).

n. f. P. hisht free. Paradise; Heaven; the abode of the blest. bahisht kā mevah , n. m. ( Met .) A pomegranate. bahisht kī qumriyāṅ , n. f. ( Met .) Dancing girls. bahisht kī havā , n. f. A cool refreshing breeze; a heavenly climate.

n. m. 1. The dwellers in Heaven; the blessed. Bahrā bahishtī, andhā dozkhī ( yā be-īmān ). Prov. The deaf for Heaven, the blind for Hell. (The blind man is very suspicious). ☞ It is related that a blind man at a feast, suspecting that the other guests might be eating with both hands, began to do likewise. It then occured to him that they might be eating with their mouths too. So he applied his mouth to the dish as well. Finally he thought that the others might be running away with their dishes. So he took up his dish and ran away. 2. A Mahomedan water bearer ( saqqā .) bāhish'tī , adj. Related to or pertaining to paradise; heavenly; celestial.
more: 1. adj. Paradisiacal, celestial. 2. s. m. A water-carrier.

def: s.m. Lands lying at a distance from the village.
v. n. 1. To flow, pass. 2. To go or swim with the stream. 3. To be ruined, to be destroyed.
s.f. Beauty, goodliness, grace, excellence; brightness, splendour; happiness, joy, gladness, delight, pleasure, cheerfulness, alacrity.
s. f. Joy, pleasure, gladness, cheerfulness, alacrity, beauty, grace, excellence.

v. a. S. वह् to carry. 1. To entice; seduce; mislead; lead astray. Banye kā bahkāyā, aur jogī kā phiṭkārā . Prov. From the banya's guile and the saint's curse there is no escape. ☞ A countryman happened to possess a gold mohur which he offered for sale. A banyā , hoping to buy it cheap from the simple fellow, offered him five rupees, which the other refused in hopes of getting more. As the banyā gradually raised his offer, the countryman's estimate of the great value of his prize and his reluctance to part with it increased, when the banyā , finding his offer of 14 Rupees was refused, changed his tactics and, assuming the part of a friend, advised him not to part with the gold mohur for less than 30 Rupees. The countryman having in vain sought a purchaser at a price which was twice the market value of the coin, finally came back to the banyā and let him have it for 14 Rupees. 2. To cause to err, blunder, slip. 3. To tempt; allure; inspire false hopes; decoy. 4. To cheat; deceive; delude. 5. To misrepresent; misstate. 6. To balk; set wrong; baffle; elude. 7. To poison one's ear; set one against another. Bahkā sikā-ke bhejā . 8. To excite; instigate; incite. Kis ne bahkāyā hai? 9. To make one chatter, jabber, babble. Tab hī lutf hai, sāqyā, maë-kashī kā, Ki tū bhī bahak, aur mujh ko bhī bahkā . Inshā. bahkā le jānā, bhagā le jānā , v. a. 1. To kidnap; carry off (a person). 2. To run away with; elope with.
more: (trans. of بـﮩکنا) v. a. To balk, disappoint, mislead, deceive, beguile.

def: v.t. To intimidate, work upon one's fears; to frighten away; to to lead away, mislead, beguile, deceive, delude; to cause to err, blunder, or slip; to baulk, disappoint; to entice, allure, seduce, decoy; to stir up, excite, incite, instigate; to set (one) against; to make one forget himself, to intoxicate; to make one chatter or babble
subs: v.t. To entice or lure away; to run away with, carry off, elope with.

r: bahkāwā, bahkāwaṭ, bahkāyā, ruse, bahkāwe-meṅ ānā, ke
def: s.m. = H بہکاوٿ बहकावट bahkāwaṭ, s.f. = H بہکايا बहकाया bahkāyā, s.m. Intimidation; frightening off or away; misleading, leading astray; imposition, deception, delusion; blind, feint, trick, stratagem, ruse
subs: (-ke), To be intimidated (by); to fall into the snare or allurement (of), to be deceived or deluded (by), be imposed upon (by); &c.

def: n. m. 1. Deception; imposition; blind; feint; delusion. Kiske bahkāë meṅ ā-kar laṛte ho? 2. Stratagem; trick; trickery; circumvention; dodge; ruse ; hoax; fetch; make-believe. bahkāve meṅ ānā, bahkāë sikāë meṅ ānā , v. n. To be deceived, imposed upon, deluded.

r: bahkāwaṭ, bahkāwā, bahkāyā, ruse, bahkāwe-meṅ ānā, ke
def: s.f. = H بہکاوا बहकावा bahkāwā, s.m. = H بہکايا बहकाया bahkāyā, s.m. Intimidation; frightening off or away; misleading, leading astray; imposition, deception, delusion; blind, feint, trick, stratagem, ruse
subs: (-ke), To be intimidated (by); to fall into the snare or allurement (of), to be deceived or deluded (by), be imposed upon (by); &c.

r: bahkāyā, bahkāwā, bahkāwaṭ, ruse, bahkāwe-meṅ ānā, ke
def: s.m. = H بہکاوا बहकावा bahkāwā, s.m. = H بہکاوٿ बहकावट bahkāwaṭ, s.f. Intimidation; frightening off or away; misleading, leading astray; imposition, deception, delusion; blind, feint, trick, stratagem, ruse
subs: (-ke), To be intimidated (by); to fall into the snare or allurement (of), to be deceived or deluded (by), be imposed upon (by); &c.

def: adj. Little; easy; — s.m. Malediction, curse.
adj. Barren (generally applied to cows).
v.t. To carry water into a field, to flood a field.

v. a. S. विहरण sporting. 1. To amuse; divert; entertain. Phr. To beguile or while away the time. 1. Rāt hai to kyā huā, kokā, dugānā ko bulā, Khush nahīṅ ātā mujhe is dam yeh bahlānā terā . Raṅgīṅ. 2. Ṭukṛe khāë. din bahlāe, Kapṛe phāṭe, ghar ko āë . Prov. On crusts of bread abroad he'll roam, Tattered and torn he'll come back home. 2. To cheer; enliven; solace. 3. To carry water into a field.
(trans. of بـﮩلنا) v. a. To divert, to amuse, to recreate.
v.t. To cause to be pleased, to amuse, divert, recreate, entertain, cheer, enliven (syn. bahirānā).
more: v. a. Caus. of بُهولنا q. v. 1. To mislead; lead astray. 2. To divert. (بـﮩـلانا).

def: n. m. Amusement; diversion.
s.f. A small two-wheeled vehicle without springs, drawn by two oxen ( = bahal).
n. f. S. वह to carry. A small two-wheeled vehicle without springs, drawn by two oxen.
s.m. Name of a small tribe of Mohammadans near Mīraṭ and Dasna.
s.m. Driver of a bahlī ( = bahal-wān).

v. n. To be diverted, cheered, etc. Piawā ke khojata phirūṅ ham nagre chhāṅdi chhāṅdi jas bilārī ho, Rāmā, Hār thak-ke paralūṅ aganwā, tinoṅ bhavan meṅ sansā na bahlal ho, Rāmā . Bhoj. Song. My love I sought cat-like from roof to roof the town, O Ram! Tired out, stretched in the yard, relief three worlds none gave, O Rām.
more: v. n. To be diverted, to be amused.

def: s. m. 1. A prince endowed with every virtue. 2. Name of a celebrated Dervis. 3. A man much addicted to laughter.
s.m. A prince endowed with every virtue; one who is much addicted to laughter; jester, joker; name of a celebrated dervish.
s.m. = brāhman, q.v.
more: s. m. A Brāhman, a man of the sacerdotal class of Hindūs.
subs: s.f. = bāman-haṭṭī, q.v. s.m. A Brāhmaṇ, a member of the first Hindū class.

ety: Pehl. vahu-man; Zend vohu-manaṅh; S. वसु मनस्
def: s.m. The eleventh solar month (when the sun is in Aquarius); — name of Ardshīr, son of Isfandyār; — name of a plant which flowers in the month bahman (it is distinguished, from the colour of the root, into two kinds, surḵẖ, 'red,' and sufaid 'white').
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بهمن (bahman), ultimately from Old Persian *Vahumanah (literally “good mind”).

def: s.m. = brāhman, q.v. s.m. A Brāhmaṇ, a member of the first Hindū class.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بهمن (bahman), ultimately from Old Persian *Vahumanah (literally “good mind”).

def: s. The eleventh solar month, when the sun is in Aquarius.
more: s. m. A Brāhman. See براهمن.

def: s.m. = brāhmaṇ-eṭā, q.v. s.m. A young Brāhmaṇ.
s.m. A young Brāhmaṇ ( = brāhman-eṭā, bāhman-eṭā).
s. m. A young Brāhman.
s.f. = brāhmaṇī, q.v. s.f. A Brahmaness, — wife of a Brahman; a woman of the Brāhmaṇical caste.
more: s. f. The wife of a Brāhman, a Brāhmaness. See بامهني bāmhnī.

def: adj. Of or relating to a Brahman; relating to religious worship; sacred
subs: s.f. The sacred or Brāhmanī kite.

def: v.t. To lay open, unfold, open, spread; to expose, show (the teeth, &c.); to open out, comb (the hair); to plough, till; to drudge, toil, labour; to carry on, drive, prosecute, to shoot, discharge (a weapon); to pierce, penetrate, drive in; to cover (an animal), impregnate.
more: (caus. of بهنا To flow, q. v.) v. a. To shoot, to discharge a weapon.
v. a. To plough, to till.

def: v.n. To flow; to go or swim with the stream; to float; to drift; to glide, slide; to run (as a sore); to blow (as a breeze); to pass, drive; to be carried away, be separated; to be lost, ruined, or destroyed; to be wasted, squandered, or dissipated; to melt, dissolve, become watery
more: v. n. भस्ज् to fry. 1. To be fried, parched. Chane bhun gaë . 2. To burn (mentally); ache; bleed; grieve. bhunāī, bhunvāī , n. f. The price paid for parching grain. bhūnī bhāṅg , n. f. Fried hemp leaves. 1. Bhūnī bhāṅg, na kaṛvā tel . Prov. Nor hemp nor oil. 2. Garmī sabzah-rangoṅ se, aur ghar meṅ bhūnī bhāṅg nahīṅ! Prov. A worshipper of Venus warm, and in his house no warming bhāṅg!
v. n. S. भंज् to break. To be changed (money). Rupayā bhun gayā. bhunā'ī, bhunvāī , W; bhanjat, bhujāī , E. n. f. Discount, or loss in exchange.
subs: v.n. = bahnā (esp. in an intens. sense); to run off or away; to blow away, &c.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀳𑀤𑀺 (vahadi), from Sanskrit वहति (váhati).

def: v. a. 1. To spread; open; expand. Mūṅh bānā . 2. To plough; till; cultivate. 3. To work; labour; toil; fag. 4. To prosecute; pursue; carry on. 5. To comb (the hair). Bāl bāhnā . 6. To show one's teeth. Dāṅt bāhnā . 7. To penetrate; drive in; thrust in ( bāṛnā ). 8. To cover; cause to conceive ( būnā ).
n. m. Sisterhood.
s.m. Sisterly affection or kindness, sisterliness; sisterhood.
Sisterly affection, &c. ( = bahnāpā, q.v.); friendship among women
n. m. H. bahn sister. Sisterhood; friendship among females. bahnelā joṛnā , v. n. Wom. To form a friendship.
s.f. Adopted sister; female friend (among women).

n. f. An adopted sister; a friend. Ek bahan thī, ek bahnelī, yoṅhī bahut din guṛyāṅ khelī Jis se maiṅ kahtī tamāshā dikhā lā, un ne uṭhā-ke god meṅ le lī . Wom. Zafar. A sister and a playmate once I had, And many a livelong day with dolls did play, And sports were shown me by whomso'er I bad, Too glad to take me in their arms each day.
s. m. A sisterʼs husband, a brother-in-law.
s.m. = bahnoʼī, q.v. s.m. Sister's husband, brother-in-law.

n. f. S. वहङ्ग from वह to carry. A pole, with slings at either end, supporting portable boxes or baskets for baggage, carried over one shoulder. bahṅgī bar-dār,—wālā ; E. bahiṅgwār , n. m. A bahṅgī or baggage bearer or porter.
more: s. f. A stick with ropes hanging from each end for slinging baggage to, which is carried on the shoulder.

def: n. f. S. भगिनीपति Sister's husband ( jījā ); brother-in-law. Apne bahnoī se nahīṅ boltā? Go and talk to your superior (brother-in-law).
s. m. A sisterʼs husband, a brother-in-law.
s.m. Sister's husband, brother-in-law.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀩𑀳𑀺𑀡𑀯𑀇 (*bahiṇavaï), from Sanskrit भगिनीपति (bhaginīpati), from भगिनी (bhaginī, “sister”) + पति (páti, “husband”).

def: s.m. Sister's son, nephew (syn. bhānjā).
n. m. A sister's son ( bhānjā ).
s.m. Land given rent-free to the watchman (ćaukīdār) of a village (cf. bhūṅḍarī).
s.m. = bohrā, q.v. s.m. A class of village bankers or money-lenders; an individual of that class. (The Bohrās are Mohammadans, who came originally from Guzrāt)
more: adj. Brown. Bhūrā bainsā, chandlī joë, Pās mahāvaṭ birle hoë . Prov. A brown buffalo, a bald wife, and rain in December are three things rare.

def: s.m. Food sent by the bride's family for the bridegroom and his friends when the bride is about to leave home, or along with the marriage procession; the ceremony of the bride's dismissal with her husband.
adv. = bahuri, q.v. adv. Again, a second time.
more: n. f. bhū earth, ret sand. Soil of the fourth quality; sandy or porous soil. Bhūṛ ke hūṛ hote haiṅ . Prov. Unproductive soil, consisting, for the most part, of seven tenths of sand and the rest of clay, with very little vegetable mould, being the third in common enumeration. Varieties of it are termed bhūḍ khākī and bhūd bārānī .

def: s.f. Decking oneself (like a bride, bahū), &c. = banāʼo, q.v. s.m. Preparation, formation, composition, fabric, &c. ( = banāwaṭ, q.v.); dressing, preparing, decking (oneself); decoration, embellishment, adornment; good understanding, harmony, concord, mutual agreement (of persons, things, or qualities), reconciliation; adjustment, amendment, correction, remedy
subs: (-kā), To adorn, bedeck, ornament, decorate, embellish
(-se), A good understanding to exist (between or with), to be on good terms, get on well (together).

def: adv. = bahuri, q.v. adv. Again, a second time.
v.t. = bahurnā, and bahurānā; qq.v. v.n. To come again, come back, return.
s.m. The sloping pathway for bullocks drawing water from a well (by which they return to the well).
s.m. Sea, gulf, say; — s.f. Metre, verse; flow, rhythm; — fleet (of ships or boats).
more: 1. s. m. A sea, a bay, a gulph. 2. s. f. Metre, verse. 3. A fleet.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بحر (bahr), from Arabic بَحْر (baḥr).

def: prep. For, on account of. ¶ ba-har, In every, to every, &c.
more: (corruption of بحر) s. f. A fleet.

def: prep. On account of, for the sake of, for.
s.m. Property, fortune; share, portion, quota ( = baḵẖra, q.v.); profit, gain, advantage

def: n. f. S. वह् to carry, to marry. 1. Wife; a son's wife; daughter-in-law; a bride. 1. Ghī saṅvāre kām, baṛī bahū kā nām . Prov. The flavour from the butter came, the elder daughter-in-law yet gets the name. 2. Jabtak bahū kuārī, tab tak sās wārī, Bahū āī god meṅ, lāḍ gayā haud meṅ . Prov. A maid unwed, the mother-in-law to be devoted see, As daughter-in-law embraced, her love and fondness out will flee. 3. Jān līyo bas ham ne, bahūaṛ, Tū bhī khūb pichhāne sai, Jaisī terī hotī aī vaisā hamnū jāne sai! W. Rus. Song. Yes, daughter-in-law, I know you now, Your penetration's great, I vow, As you've been bred you take me so! 4. Sās kahe 'merī āī bohaṛyā, jī apne ko wārūṅgī,' 'Tū hī, sāsū, pānī bhar lā, to se hī piswāūṅgī .' Rus. Wom. Song. The mother-in-law says, 'now a daughter-in-law I've got, To her my life I would devote.' Says she 'now ma, you shall go fill the water pot, And grind the corn for us, I vote.' 2. A boy's penis. bahū beṭī,—beṭyāṅ , n. f. 1. Wives and daughters. 2. Ladies; gentlewomen. Bahū, beṭī sab rakhte haiṅ . Prov. saying. All have wives and daughters. n. f. from Marahṭī. वायको a woman. 1. A lady; mistress; madam. Bīvī rī, tū bāī, chaṅge din āī, Tere jīveṅ bahan aur bhāī. Lorī . Lullaby. Sweet baby mine, a lady fine, good days incline, Long may your brother's and your sister's countenance shine! 2. E. A dancing girl; prostitute; the mother bawd; the old abbess; " la bonne " ( nāëkā ).
n. f. from H. bāo wind. Rheumatism ( bāoṭā , 4). Pāoṅ meṅ bāī ā gaī .
s. f. 1. Mistress, lady (among Mahārāttas). 2. A dancing girl. بائي جي bāʼī jī, s. f. The mother bawd.
subs: s.f. Mother bawd, the old abbess (syn. nāyikā).

def: s.f. Side of a bedstead, &c. (syn. paṭṭī).
s.f. Buying and selling, commerce; selling; sale
more: n. f. بيع selling, buying. Sale; buying and selling. bai bāt . Foreclosure of mortgage; absolute sale; unconditional sale. bai bil-wafā ; H. jākaṛ bikrī , n. f. 1. Conditional sale or mortgage. 2. A contract of pawn or usufruct. bai jāiz yā haqīqī ; A valid sale. bai-khāngī ; H. gharelū bikrī , n. f. Private sale. bai sāzishī , n. f. A collusive sale. bai sultānī ; H. rāj-bikrī , n. f. A Government sale. bai fāsid ; H. jhūṭī bikrī , n. f. A void sale. bai farebī ; H. dhoke kī bikrī , n. f. A fraudulent sale. bai kāmil yā qataī, bai shartī yā kaṭ qibālā, bai gair mashrūt, bai mutlaq, bai lā-kalāmī, bai qataī o gair mushrūta ; H. pakkī bikrī , n. f. Foreclosure of mortgage; absolute sale; unconditional or unrestricted sale; an out-and-out sale. bai karnā ; H. bechnā , v. a. To sell; alienate; transfer. bai muqāsāt' , Property in place of marriage. bai mīādī . A conditional sale. bai-nā-jāi…
s. f. (Buying and selling) Commerce, selling. بيع کرنا baiﻌ karnā, v. a. To sell.
subs: s.f. Foreclosure of mortgage; absolute or unconditional sale
s.f. Conditional sale or mortgage; contract of pawn or usufruct; — baiʻ biʼl wafā-dār, s.m. A person having the possession and usufruct of a property on its conditional sale to him
s.m. Lease or sub-lease obtained by purchase
s.f. Sale by order of government
s.f. Sale which is null and void
s.m. Deed of sale; bill of sale; certificate of sale ( = qabāla-ě-nīlāmī)
s.m. Buying and selling; sale and purchase; transaction (syn. ḵẖarīd-o-faroḵẖt).

ety: S. वायु; cf. bāʼo = S. वात
def: s.f. Wind, air; flatulency; rheumatism; delirium, raving
subs: bai-birang, bā-birang, bābrang (S. vāyu+viḍanga), s.m. A medicinal seed, Embelia ribes, employed to remove flatulency or rheumatism ( = bāʼo-birang, q.v.): bāʼī paćnā, v.n. To be mortified; to be depressed; to die away
v.n. To break wind ( = bāʼo sarnā)
v.n. To prate, talk foolishly; to rave in delirium (N.B. — For compounds not found in this place, see s.v. bāʼo).

def: s.m. Earnest-money; advance
more: n. m. Earnest money; advance. bayānā denā , v. a. To give money in advance; bind a bargain.
subs: (-ko), To give earnest-money, give money in advance, to bind a bargain.

def: s.f. The act of promising or swearing allegiance and obedience; submission, obedience, allegiance, homage, fealty; initiation as a disciple of a saint or religious guide
more: n. f. The initiation of one as a disciple of some saint or religious guide.
s. f. Homage, fealty. بيعت کرنا baiʼaﻌt karnā, v. a. To become a disciple of some saint.
subs: (-kī), To take the oath of allegiance, &c.; to become a disciple of some saint.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَیعَت (bay'at), borrowed from Arabic بَيْعَة (bayʕa). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi بیعت (by't /⁠bai'at⁠/).

def: adv. At a distance, afar off.
s.f. The Bible.
s.m. Power, wealth, grandeur, greatness, pre-eminence.
adj. Far, far off, distant, remote
more: (from بعد) adj. Far, distant, absent, remote; strange, improbable. بعيدعقل سي baʼīﻌd aﻌḳl se, Foolish, absurd, far from wise, unreasonable.
subs: baʻīd aql-se, adj. Far removed from reason, unreasonable, improbable, foolish, absurd (syn. budh-biparīt)
adj. & s.m. Far from home; — a foreigner.

def: s.m. Man of the medical caste, physician.
more: n. m. S. वैद्य from विद् to know. A physician; a quack. 1. Jā, baidā, ghar āpne, tū kī jāne sār? Āshaq chaṅge kin kīye bin dekhe dīdār? Dohā. Go home, physician, go, what know you of my case? Was ever lover cured—unseen the loved one's face? 2. Āṅtā tītā, dāṅtā non, peṭ bharan ko tīnhī kon, Āṅkhe pānī, kānhe tel, kahe Ghāg, baidāī gel . Prov. Take bitter for your guts, and salt your gums, your stomach three parts fill, put water to your eyes, oil in your ears—physician then may go.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit वैद्य (vaidya).

def: n. m. Riot; tumult; uproar; mutiny.
s.f. Practice of physic.
s. f. Practice of physick.
s.f. The practice or science of physic ( = baidāʼī).
more: n. f. The practice or science of physic.

def: s.m. The pawn at chess.
more: (from Pers. پياده) s. m. A pawn at chess.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَیدَق (baydaq), from Arabic بَيْدَق (baydaq), back-formation from بَيَادِق (bayādiq), from Middle Persian 𐭯𐭣𐭠𐭲𐭪' (pdʾtk' /⁠payādag, payādak⁠/). Doublet of پِیادَہ (piyāda).

def: adj. & s.m. Acquainted with or versed in the Vedas; — a reader of the Vedas; a Brāhmaṇ well versed in the Vedas.
more: n. m. A reader of the Veda; a Brahman well-versed in the Vedās.

def: s. m. A Brāhmaṇ well versed in the Vedas. ¶ baidak ( वैद्यक ) s. m. The practice or science of physick.
n. m. P. bad bad. 1. A bad character ( badmaāsh ); a petty thief. 2. A gambler ( jwārī ).
s.m. = baidak, q.v. s.m. The pawn at chess.

In abundance.

more: s. m. The egg plant. (Solanum melongena).

s.f. Rheumatism ( = bāʼī, q.v.). s.f. Lady, mistress, madam; woman, wife (local; cf. bāʼīkā); — dancing-girl, harlot; mother bawd, la bonne
more: s. m. Rheumatism. See بات वात
subs: s.f. Mother bawd, the old abbess (syn. nāyikā).

def: s.f. Flag, standard, banner; the standard of Vishnu
more: s. f. A flag or standard; the standard of Vish̤ṇu.
subs: baijantī-mālā, s.f. A kind of garland; the necklace of Vishnu (composed of five gems produced from the five elements of nature, viz. the sapphire, from the earth; the pearl, from water; the ruby, from fire; the topaz, from air; and the diamond, from space or æther).

def: n. f. S. वैजयन्ती from विजि to conquer. A necklace worn by Vishnu in his several incarnations. It was believed to be composed of the following jewels produced from the five elements, viz . the sapphire from earth, the pearl from water, the ruby from fire, the topaz from air, and the diamond from space or ether.
more: s. f. A necklace especially worn by Vish̤ṇu in his several forms, and composed of the following jewels, produced from the five elements of nature: namely, the sapphire, from the earth; the pearl, from water; the ruby, from fire; the topaz, from air; and, the diamond, from space or ether.

def: s.m. A species of black pulse.
s. f. (dakh.) Woman: see باٴيکو bāʼīko.
s.f. Woman, wife. (The word is peculiar to Western India: — the Marahṭī form is बायकी).
s.m. Afternoon, evening ( = bikāl).
more: s. m. Afternoon. See بکال bikāl.

def: s.f. Woman, wife. (The word is peculiar to Western India: — the Marahṭī form is बायकी).
s.m. A name of Vishnu; the paradise or heaven of Vishnu; Paradise (syn. bihisht)
subs: s.m. Dweller of Paradise; deceased; — baikuṇṭh-bāshī honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To become a dweller in Paradise, to go to heaven; to die.

def: n. m. S. वैकुण्ठ Heaven; paradise. baikunṭh-bāshī honā , v. n. To go to heaven; to die. Lālā jī baikunṭh-bāshī ho gaë .
s. m. 1. A name of Vish̤ṇu. 2. The heaven or paradise of Vish̤ṇu.
s.m. Bullock, ox; (Astron.) Taurus; (met.) Blockhead, fool
more: n. m. S. बालिन् or बलीवर्द. 1. An ox; bull; bullock. 1. Bail na kūdā, kūdī gon, yeh tamāshā dekhe kaun? Prov. The pannier jumped without the ox, who ever saw such a paradox? (Said to one who obtrudes himself where he is not required.) 2. Mail kā bail . Prov. A peck of dirt to a bullock grew. (A mountain of a mole hill). 2. ( Ast .) Taurus (A. saur ). 3. ( Met .) A blockhead; fool; (as the Scotch say) "a stol." Kaisā hī ādmī ho, par iflās ke tufaīl, Koī gadhā kahe, koī ṭhairāë usko bail . On Poverty. Nazīr. The best of men, if poor he only be, Is dubbed an ass, or silly ox may be. bail begārī , Bullocks impressed for the public service. bail-gāṛī , n. f. A bullock cart.
s. m. A bull, an ox. ( met. ) A blockhead.
subs: s.m. Bullocks impressed for the public service
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑁂𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀻 (vĕllī), from Sanskrit वेल्लि (velli). Cognate with Gujarati વેલ (vel), Marathi वेल (vel), Punjabi ویل (vel) / ਵੇਲ (vel).

def: adj. Barren (a cow).
s.m. A species of bird.
s.f. = bahlī, q.v. s.f. A small two-wheeled vehicle without springs, drawn by two oxen ( = bahal).

adj. Left = bāṅyāṅ, q.v., baiṅ-hatthā, adj. Left-handed. adj. Left (hand) = bāyāṅ, bāṅwāṅ, q.q.v. adj. (f. bāʼīṅ) & s.m. Left; — the left hand ( = bāṅwān, bāṅyāṅ); (in music) the bass; the bass sound (particularly of the t̤abla, ḍholak, and pakhāwaj, which are played with the left hand)
more: n. f. Sound; word. See باني
subs: v.n. To have an omen from the left-hand side (which is regarded as good)
(or pair) pūjnā (-kā), lit. 'To worship the left leg (of)'; to acknowledge the superiority or cleverness or cunning (of)
bāṅʼeṅ, bāṅweṅ, adv. To the left; on the left-hand side.

def: prep. Separation, interval, distance between; — between, among
subs: adj. Betwixt and between, intermediate (between good and evil, &c.); middling, mediocre, passable, tolerable, indifferent; —

s.m. An ornament (consisting of a small circular plate of gold with enamel work) worn by women on the forehead.
more: s. m. An ornament worn on the forehead.
s. m. Sweetmeats, cakes, &c. distributed at marriages and other ceremonies. ¶ benā (for वीरण ) s. m. Name of a grass, ḵẖas (Andropogon muricatum). ¶ benā (for वैणव ) s. m. A fan.

def: adj. Intermediate (between good and evil); middling, passable, indifferent, tolerable.
s.m. Mark placed on the forehead by Hindūs preparatory to devotion (syn. tilak; ṭiklī; cf. bain, baiṅdī; būṅdā).
more: adj. S. वण्ड crooked. 1. Crooked; difficult; hard. 1. Bīṅḍā balad nā maiṅ rāhhūṅ, ūṅch sīṅgyā lāūṅgī . W. Rus. song. I will not have an ox with crooked horn, an ox I'll bring with long straight horn. 2. Baṛā baiṅḍā kām haị, samajh meṅ nahīṅ ātā . 2. Rude; rough; impolite; uncivilized. baiṅḍī sunānā , v. a. 1. To speak harshly; give crooked answers. 2. To revile; insult ( aiṅḍī baiṅḍī sunānā ).
s. m. A mark placed on the forehead as a preparative to devotion among the Hindūs. See ٿکلي and تلک.

def: s.f. Throwing up water with a basket from a pond or reservoir for the purpose of irrigation.
s.f. An ornamental circlet made with a coloured earth or unguent on the forehead and between the eyebrows; an ornament or fillet worn by women on the forehead ( = bain, q.v.).
more: n. f. S. बेणी 1. Twisted or plaited hair. 2. A coil. bīṅḍī yā meṅḍyāṅ gūṅdhnā , v. n. To plait or braid the hair.
n. Throwing up water from a pond or reservoir with a basket for irrigation.
s. f. 1. An ornamental circlet made with a coloured earth or unguent on the forehead and between the eyebrows. 2. An ornament worn by women on the forehead.

def: s.m. The egg-plant, Solanum melongena, and its fruit, brinjal ( = baigan).
more: ( Fac .) be-gun (without merit), n. m. S. वङ्गन P. bādanjān . The egg-plant; Solanum metongena ; brinjal. Khā, to baiṅgan se, na khā to baiṅgan se. Double entendre . Eat bread with brinjal, or suck my thumb. thālī meṅ kā baigan , n. m. A man of fickle disposition.
s. m. The egg plant. See بيگن baigan.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀸𑀇𑀁𑀕𑀡 (vāiṃgaṇa), from Sanskrit वातिङ्गण (vātiṅgaṇa), from Proto-Dravidian *waẓVtV. Doublet of بادنجان (bādanjān).

def: adj. Of the coloar of baiṅgan, purple ( = baiṅjnī).
s.f. = bahangī, q.v
s.f. A basket in which snakes are carried about for show.

def: adj. Purple = baiṅgnī, q.v.

(from بينگن) adj. Purple (the colour of the Solanum melongena).

def: s.m. A subdivision of the Gūjar tribe.
s.m. = benṭ.

n. m. The handle (of knives, spades, etc.). Bīṅṭā mūl saṅbhaltā nāhīṅ, kaise jāṛī khodūṅ rī, Khurpā khunḍā chaltā nāhīṅ, kaise jāṛī khodūṅ rī . Wom. Rus. song. The handle grasp I cant, the grass I cannot dig, This iron scraper's blunt, the grass I cannot dig.
s.m. Traffic, trade, merchandise; business, labour
more: s. m. Traffick, trade, business, labour.

def: s.m. Trader, merchant.
s.m. Enmity, hostility, animosity, ill-will, hatred, revenge, malice, rancour
more: n. f. S. बैर Pr. vaīram . 1. Enmity; hostility; antipathy; malice; ill-will; animosity. 1. Huqqe aur bātoṅ meṅ bair hai . Prov. Smoking and talking adverse are. 2. Daryā meṅ rahnā, aur magar machh se bair! Prov. Live in the river, and at feud with the crocodile! 3. Kabīr khaṛā bajār meṅ, doū dīn kī khair, Nā kāhū se dostī, nā kāhū se bair . Kabir. Kabīr stands in the mart, to both creeds equally inclined, Nor friendship with the one, nor feud with t' other in his mind. 2. Hate; hatred; abhorrence; detestation. bair bāṅdhnā, bisānā, karnā, yā rakhnā , v. a. To hate; dislike; bear malice; owe a grudge. 1. Achchhā kīyā jo āp ne bāṅdhā hai ham se bair, Jīte rahe to samjheṅge, aur mar gaë to khair . 2. Māṅ sūṅ kākī bheṛ rakhe saë, ghar nyārā kar le bāppū, Yo maiṅ sīkh det sūṅ āchhī, tauṅ hirde dhar le bāppū . Rus. Song. 3. Hāth kā denā, aur bair bisānā . Prov. Who money lends oft loses friend…
s. m. Enmity, hostility, animosity, revenge. بيرلينا bair lenā, v. a. To revenge.
subs: bair bisānā, bair rakhnā, bair karnā (-se), To be at enmity (with); to bear a grudge (against), to bear malice, to hate
s.m. Provoker of enmity; bearer of malice, one who owes a grudge; enemy
s.m. Hostile relations; (wife's) natural enemies (viz. the saut or co-wife; the nand or husband's sister; the sās or mother-in-law; the sisters-in-law; and the other relations)
bair nikālnā (apnā), To take revenge, avenge oneself, to retaliate
bair-yukt, adj. Inimical, hostile, revengeful, malicious.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیر (bīr), from Middle Persian wyl (wīr), from Proto-Indo-European *wiHrós. Cognate with Avestan 𐬬𐬍𐬭𐬀 (vīra), Sanskrit वीर (vīra, “man (especially a brave one), chief, husband”), Northern Kurdish wêrek (“courageous”), Kashmiri ویٖر (vīr, “hero”), Ossetian ир (ir), Latin vir (“man”) Lithuanian výras, and English wer.

def: s.m. Bearer; valet.
more: n. m. Cor. of Bearer. A valet; a palanquin-bearer.

def: adj. With the intention or design, intending.
s.m. Freedom from passion or from worldly attachments, subjection of the appetites; renunciation of worldly pleasures, seclusion from the world; devotion, austerity, penance
more: n. m. S. वैराग्य from वि neg, राग passion. Renunciation of the pleasures of the world; seclusion from the world; austerity. bairāg lenā , v. a. To retire from the world; renounce the pleasures of the world; lead the life of a devotee; become a recluse in consequence of unrequited love.
s. m. Penance, devotion, the act of leaving the pleasures of the world.
subs: To withdraw from the world, renounce the pleasures of the world; to lead the life of an ascetic; to become a recluse in consequence of unrequited love.

more: n. m. A horizontal stick placed upon another vertical stick, resembling somewhat a crosier, which a Bairāgi places under his armpit to lean upon as he sits to his devotion.
s. m. A small crooked stick, which a bairāgī places under his arm-pit to lean upon as he sits.
s. m. A small crooked stick or piece of iron, which the bairāgī places under his arm-pit to lean upon as he sits ( Qanoon-e-islam, p. 295).

def: s.f. = H بيراگا बैरागा bairāgā, s.m. A small cross-shaped stick or piece of iron which a Bairāgī places under his arm-pit to lean upon as he sits.
more: n. f. 1. A female bairāgī . 2. A small crooked stick having two ears on the top.
s. f. 1. A female bairāgī. 2. Same as بيراگا q. v.

def: s.f. The wife of a bairāgī; a female bairāgī.

adj. & s.m. Separated from passion or worldly attachments; austere, recluse; — one who has subdued his worldly desires and passions, one who abandons the pursuits of this world; ascetic, devotee, stoic, recluse; a class of Hindū faqīrs who roam the country and practise certain austerities.
more: n. m. One who has subduced his worldly desires and passions and retired from the world; an ascetic or recluse. At present the term is applied to a class of Hindu faqirs who roam the country and practise certain austerities. Bairāgī bairāg chhoṛ-ke pāp karam karne jāveṅ, Lobh, moh meṅ phase sab, sādh, sant jo kahlāveṅ . Song. Lāonī. Kaljug . The saint his saintship doffs, with sin his soul attaints, In greed and lust all sunk, both perfect men and saints.
s. m. 1. The religious ascetick, or he who abandons terrestrial objects, thoughts, passions, &c. 2. A kind of wandering faḳīr who practices certain austerities.

def: s.f. The wife of a bairāgī; a female bairāgī.
s.m. bairāg, q.v.
s.m. = bairaq, q.v.
s.m. Flag, standard, banner, pennant, ensign; flag set up on taking possession of new or unoccupied land; (hence) taking possession of such land, or official authority to do so.
more: n. m. A flag. More especially a flag set up on taking possession of new or unoccupied land, whence it has come to signify the act of taking possession of such land, or official authority to do so.

s.m. Hostile relations; (wife's) natural enemies (viz. the saut or co-wife; the nand or husband's sister; the sās or mother-in-law; the sisters-in-law; and the other relations)

def: s.f. Female enemy (see bairī).
more: n. f. 1. A female enemy. 2. Met . A co-wife ( saut ). Jo tum saīyāṅ Dakhanvā ko jaīho, Sautan mat laīyo re, bairan mat laiyo! Wom. Song. If you, my husband, southern climes must go, O bring home no co-wife, oh! bring home no foe!

def: adj. Bearing (postage), unpaid.
s.m. Enemy, foe, adversary.
more: n. m. 1. An enemy; a foe. 2. ( bahrī , n. f.). A female hawk; a falcon.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian باعث (bā'is), from Arabic بَاعِث (bāʕiṯ).

def: adj. Twenty-two.
more: adj. S. द्वाविंशति, Pālī. dvāvīsatī, dvāvīsā, bāvīsatī , adj. Twenty-two. Sab dhān bāis paserī . E. Prov. The same rate (22 five-seers) for all grains. (To a man of no discernment good and bad are alike).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀩𑀸𑀯𑀻𑀲 (bāvīsa), from Sanskrit द्वाविंशति (dvā́viṃśati).

def: s.f. Energy, strength, vigour; time of health and strength, youth, prime of life; age, time of life, stage of existence.
more: Mag. n. f. S. वयस The prime of life. Bītal jāho, Gaurā, tohār baisawā . Mag. Song. Jatāsārī . Maiden, thy youth is fleeting fast.

s. m. 1. (for वैश्य ) The third of the four Hindū tribes or varns: see برن. 2. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts. (A prince of that tribe formerly reigned at Daunriākherā. His dominions, which extended over a great part of Oude, on the north bank of the Ganges, are still called Bais-wāṛā ). 3. (for वयस् ) Age.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀻𑀲 (vīsa), from Sanskrit विं॒श॒ति (viṃśatí).

def: s.m. The third of the four Hindū castes; a man of that caste (whose chief duties are agriculture and trade; hence) agriculturist; trader; — name of a tribe of Rājpūts
subs: (S. vaiśyā+vāṭika), s.m. The residence of Bais (a prince of the bais tribe); the nativity of Bais. (The province of Baiswāṛā is situated in Oudh, and is so named from a tribe of Rājpūts named Bais who formerly resided there. A prince of the tribe formerly reigned at Dunḍiyā-khera, and his dominions extended over a great part of the province of Oudh, on the north bank of the Ganges).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀻𑀲 (vīsa), from Sanskrit विं॒श॒ति (viṃśatí).

def: s.m. = bais, q.v. s.m. (f. ?), Occasion, cause, reason, motive, incentive; subject, author; — postposn. Because (of, -ke), by reason (of); on account (of), by means (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیش (bēš).

def: part. adj. Seated; settled, &c. = baiṭhā, q.v. part. adj. Seated, sitting; settled, settled down; fallen in (as a roof, &c.); — s.m. Paddle (for rowing a boat)
subs: v.n. To remain sitting, to sit; to give up (attempting a thing), to desist
adv. Sitting quietly; while comfortably seated: in a state of ease and comfort; without any trouble, labour, or exertion; easily
adv. = baiṭhe-biṭhāʼe, q.v.; (also) through continued sitting idle or unoccupied, through inactivity

def: s.f. A female of the bais caste.
s.f. (prov.), Sitting, seat; — a spot in a jungle to which cattle are sent out to graze, grazing ground; — adj. & s.m.f. (prov.), Old, worn out; ill, sick; — old and worn out animal.
more: n. A grazing field for cattle.
Rus. adj. Ill; sick. Ūṅṭ hār mhārā paṛ gayā baisak, Khep bharan jāūṅ kaise? W. Rus. Song.
s. f. (dakh.) Sitting, seat: see نشست nishast, and بيٿهک baiṭhak.

def: s.m. The first of the twelve months constituting the Hindū solar year (answering to April-May).
s.m. = baisākh, q.v. s.m. The first of the twelve months constituting the Hindū solar year (answering to April-May).
n. m. S. बैशाख, Pr. vaīshāho . The first month of the luni-solar year of the Hindus (April-May).
more: s. m. The first solar month of the Hindūs, the full moon of which is near विशाखा (four stars in Libra and Scorpio); April-May.

def: s.m. A club consecrated to the month Baisākh; a club bound with iron; a crutch (syn. bairāgā).
more: s. m. 1. A crutch. 2. A club armed with iron. 3. ( वैशाखी ) A club consecrated in the month of baisākh.

def: adj. & s.f. Of or relating to the month Baisākh; growing in Baisākh; — the day of full moon in the month of Baisākh; the crops of Baisākh; — a large kind of myrobalan or citron; — a club; a crutch ( = baisākhā).
more: 1. adj. Growing in the month of baisākh, or relating to that month. 2. s. f. A large kind of myrobalan or citron fruit. 3. The day of full moon in the month baisākh.

def: n. m. 1. The crops of Baisākh . 2. A large myrobalan or citron. 3. A wooden prop or support; crutch; club. 4. A fool; an ass. Āo, baisākhī nandan. baisā'khī , adj. Growing in baisākh , or relating to it.
s.m. Epithet of Agni or fire; fire.
more: s. m. Fire, or its deity.

def: (from baisnā), adj. m.f. Sitting still, sedentary, inactive, idle, lazy.
s.f. A female of the bais caste.
s.f. = bais, q.v. s.m. (f. ?), Occasion, cause, reason, motive, incentive; subject, author; — postposn. Because (of, -ke), by reason (of); on account (of), by means (of).
s.m. Hermit, anchoret.

adj. & s.m. Of or relating to Vishnu; — a follower of Vishnu; a kind of vagrant mendicant (who bears the marks and insignia of Vishnu, and recites hymns in honour of his avatārs, especially in the forms of Rāma and Kr̤ishṇa)
more: n. m. Hindus professing the preferential worship of Vishnu, of whom there are many sects. The name is commonly applied to vagrant mendicants bearing the marks and insignia of Vishnu, and repeating hymns in honor of his Avatars, especially Rāma and Krishnā.
adj. Of or relating to ( विष्णु ) Vish̤ṇu; s. m. A follower of Vish̤ṇu.
subs: (vaishnava+uttara), s.m. Lands granted rent free to the worshippers of Vishnu (especially to those of the mendicant order).

def: s.m. Follower of Vishnu.
s.f. The royal army (so called because composed of the troops of the twenty-two ṣūbas or provinces of the then Mogul empire); — a command of twenty-two thousand men
more: n. f. 1. The royal army, composed of the troops of the twenty-two Sūbās or provinces then constituting the Mogul empire. 2. A command of 22,000 men. bāīsī ṭūṭnā , v. a. To attack with one's whole force.
s. f. 1. The royal army (so called, because composed of the troops of twenty-two صوبه ṣūba ). 2. A command of 22,000 men. بائيسي ٿوٿنا bāʼīsī ṭūṭnā, To attack with oneʼs whole force.
subs: (-par), To attack with one's whole force.

def: v.t. = baiṭhānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to sit down, to cause to be seated, to seat; to cause to settle down, to settle, establish; to found; to station, fix, place; to apply; to set, plant; to calm, becalm, allay, assuage; to press down, make flat or even (also written biṭhānā; biṭhlanā, q.v.)

v.n. (prov.) To sit down, &c. = baiṭhnā, q.v.; — also = paiṭhnā, q.v. v.n. To seat oneself, sit down, be seated, be unemployed or idle; to sit, brood, incubate; to alight, settle, perch; to ride (on, -par); to subside, settle, abate; to pair, couple (a bird); to obtain employment or service; to be laid out or expended (on, -par); to visit a person in grief for the purpose of condolence; to sit in dharnā (q.v.)
subs: v.n. To sit down; to settle down; to fall in or down (a house, wall, &c.).

def: s.f. A female of the bais caste.
s.m. See s.v. bais. s.m. (f. ?), Occasion, cause, reason, motive, incentive; subject, author; — postposn. Because (of, -ke), by reason (of); on account (of), by means (of).
more: s. m. The residence of Bais, the nativity of Bais.

def: s.f. Abode, house, temple, edifice; couplet, distich, verse (in poetry)
more: (Heb. ) s. f. 1. A house, temple, edifice. 2. A couplet, distich, verse (in poetry). بيت بحشي bait-baḥs̤-ī, s. f. Criticism of poetry. بيت بندي bait-band-ī, s. f. Poetry, verse.
subs: s.m. lit. 'The house of God'; the temple of Mecca
s.m. lit. 'The sacred house'; the temple of Mecca ( = baituʼl-lāh)
s.m. The mansion of eminence; the highest mansion (of a planet, &c.)

s.m. Idol temple: baituʼl-māl, s.m. Public treasury or exchequer; escheat, property that falls to the crown through failure of heirs; the effects of one who dies intestate; confiscated property; (met.), an unfortunate fellow
bait-i-maʻmūr, s.m. A house in heaven, or in the sky, over against the qaʻba of Mecca (as believed by the Moḥammadans)
s.m. The temple or city of Jerusalem
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیت (bayt), from Arabic بَيْت (bayt).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Unlucky, unfortunate; vicious; — unlucky wight, &c.
adj. Stolen
s.m. One who practises the veterinary art, horse-doctor, farrier.
more: s. m. A horse doctor, a farrier.

def: s.f. Dry ginger (syn. soṅṭh).
more: n. f. A kind of dry ginger. Sab gun bharī baitrā soṅṭh . Prov. Dry batrā ginger possesses every good quality.

def: s.f. Name of a river which (according to the Hindūs) is to be crossed by the dead on their way into the world of spirits (like the Styx of the ancients); name of a river in Orissa.
more: s. f. 1. A river, according to the Hindūs, which is to be crossed by the dead on their way into the world of spirits, like the Styx of the ancients. 2. A river in Orissa.

def: s.f. Value of the government share of produce; amount settled on the land.
part. adj. Seated, sitting; settled, settled down; fallen in (as a roof, &c.); — s.m. Paddle (for rowing a boat)
subs: v.n. To remain sitting, to sit; to give up (attempting a thing), to desist
adv. Sitting quietly; while comfortably seated: in a state of ease and comfort; without any trouble, labour, or exertion; easily
adv. = baiṭhe-biṭhāʼe, q.v.; (also) through continued sitting idle or unoccupied, through inactivity

def: s.f. Seating; settling; allaying, &c.

n. f. 1. The act or state of sitting; posture. 2. A seat; a meeting hall; a place where people meet, sit, or converse; a receiving room; a drawing-room; bench. 3. A bottom ( peṅdā ). 4. ( baiṭhkī ) A gymnastic exercise. 5. A ceremony performed in honor of some spirit. baiṭhak denā , v. a. To make an offering at a temple or mosque in fulfilment of vow.
s.m. Chamber, hall, or house where meetings are held, assembly-room, house of relaxation, club-house; — baiṭhak denā, v.t. To make an offering at a temple or mosque in fulfilment of a vow.
more: s. f. 1. A seat, a place where people meet to sit and converse, a bench. 2. s. f. (from بيٿهنا) Act or state of sitting, sitting. 3. A kind of exercise.

def: v.t. To cause to sit down, to cause to be seated, to seat; to cause to settle down, to settle, establish; to found; to station, fix, place; to apply; to set, plant; to calm, becalm, allay, assuage; to press down, make flat or even (also written biṭhānā; biṭhlanā, q.v.)
more: (caus. of بيٿهنا q. v.) v. a. To cause to sit down, seat, set, settle, place, station, apply, plant, calm, becalm.

def: v. a. See بتَهانا

v.t. To cause to sit down, to cause to be seated, to seat; to cause to settle down, to settle, establish; to found; to station, fix, place; to apply; to set, plant; to calm, becalm, allay, assuage; to press down, make flat or even (also written biṭhānā; biṭhlanā, q.v.)
more: (caus. of بيٿهنا q. v.) v. a. To cause to sit down, seat, set, settle, place, station, apply, plant, calm, becalm.
v. a. To allay, to assuage.
